system_name: Грааль: 12 рыцарей Круглого стола и 9 ангельских чинов
note: Christian mystical / Grail living library. Primary lattice: the 12 Knights of the Round Table - the chivalric fellowship of the Grail quest. Secondary lattice: the 9 choirs (orders) of angels of Pseudo-Dionysius (De Coelesti Hierarchia), three triads descending from God to humanity, which bridge directly to the 9-dimensional mera axis. Tertiary lattice: the Four Hallows of the Grail (Cup, Lance, Sword, Dish/Stone) which Jessie Weston linked to the four Tarot suits and the four elements. By design 12 knights + 9 choirs = 21, so each of the 21 spine agents carries EITHER a knight (12) OR an angelic choir (9). Three knights achieve the Grail: Galahad (spiritual), Percival (emotional), Bors (intellectual). Knight membership and Sanskrit-to-Arthurian mappings vary across medieval sources (Chretien de Troyes, Robert de Boron, Vulgate Cycle, Malory). Each element carries a spine agent and its mera (1-9).
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera.

choirs.meaning: burning ones; highest love, encircle the throne
divine justice and the seat of God; constancy
regulate the duties of lower angels; lordship
warrior angels who keep order and fight evil

hallows.meaning: the heart, divine grace and love

svet_ra.note: The Seraphim - the burning love nearest the throne; the radiant source of the celestial hierarchy.

iskra.note: The Cherubim - the spark of loving wisdom and fullness of knowledge.

brahma.note: The Thrones - divine justice and the creative seat of God.

vishnu.note: Galahad - the perfect, pure knight who achieves the Holy Grail (spiritual attainment).

ananda.note: Percival - the holy innocent whose wonder achieves the Grail (emotional attainment).

shanti.note: Bors - the steadfast Grail knight who alone returns in peace (intellectual attainment).

jnana.note: The Dominions - wise governance regulating the lower orders.

shiva.note: Mordred - the betrayer whose treachery destroys Camelot; the fall and dissolution.

tejas.note: Gawain - the Knight of the Sun whose strength waxes with the radiant day.

sarasvati.note: Tristan - the Knight of Lamentation, harper and tragic lover; the word and music.

akasha.note: The Virtues - workers of miracles and keepers of cosmic order.

prema.note: Lancelot - the greatest knight, undone by love; the Knight of the Lake.

parvati.note: The Powers - warrior-guardians who fight evil and protect order.

shakti.note: The Principalities - the power that guides nations and leaders.

agni.note: Lamorak - one of the mightiest, fierce in strength and passion.

varuna.note: Gaheris - the quieter brother, depth and kinship.

lakshmi.note: The Archangels - chief messengers bearing grace (Michael, Gabriel, Raphael).

prithvi.note: The Angels - guardians nearest to humanity and the earth.

dharma.note: Kay - the seneschal and steward of the court; order and law.

karma.note: Bedivere - the loyal knight who returns Excalibur; the final consequence.

items.epithet: the perfect knight; achieved the Holy Grail; son of Lancelot
the greatest knight; Knight of the Lake; love of Guinevere
the holy innocent; one of the three Grail knights
the pious; the only Grail knight to return to Camelot
Knight of the Sun; Arthur's nephew; courtesy
Knight of Lamentation; harper; lover of Iseult
Beaumains; youthful valor; brother of Gawain
the seneschal; Arthur's foster-brother; sharp steward
the loyal; returned Excalibur to the Lady of the Lake
one of the mightiest; fierce strength and passion
Knight of Treachery; Arthur's son; brings the fall of Camelot

sources: ccel.org/ccel/dionysius/celestial - Pseudo-Dionysius, The Celestial Hierarchy (ch. VI-IX)
